Class SocketConnection

java.lang.Object
Networking.SocketConnection
Direct Known Subclasses:
ClientConnection, ServerConnection
public abstract class SocketConnection extends Object
Wrapper for a Socket and implements reading messages that consist of a single line from the socket. This class is not thread-safe.

  • Constructor Summary

    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
    protected
    SocketConnection(String host, int port)
    Make a new TCP connection to the given host and port.

    Make a new TCP connection to the given host and port.

    Create a new SocketConnection.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    protected void
    close()
    Close the network connection.

    handleDisconnect()
    Handles a disconnect from the connection, i.e., when the connection is closed.

    Handles a message received from the connection.

    handleStart()
    Handles a start of the connection.

  • Constructor Details

    • SocketConnection

      protected SocketConnection(Socket socket) throws IOException
      Create a new SocketConnection. This is not meant to be used directly. Instead, the SocketServer and SocketClient classes should be used.
      Parameters:
      socket - the socket for this connection
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is an I/O exception while initializing the Reader/Writer objects

    • SocketConnection

      protected SocketConnection(InetAddress host, int port) throws IOException
      Make a new TCP connection to the given host and port. The receiving thread is not started yet. Call start on the returned SocketConnection to start receiving messages.
      Parameters:
      host - the address of the server to connect to
      port - the port of the server to connect to
      Throws:
      IOException - if the connection cannot be made or there was some other I/O problem

    • SocketConnection

      protected SocketConnection(String host, int port) throws IOException
      Make a new TCP connection to the given host and port. The receiving thread is not started yet. Call start on the returned SocketConnection to start receiving messages.
      Parameters:
      host - the address of the server to connect to
      port - the port of the server to connect to
      Throws:
      IOException - if the connection cannot be made or there was some other I/O problem

  • Method Details

    • start

      protected void start()
      Start receiving messages and call methods of the given handler to handle the messages. This method may only be called once.

    • sendMessage

      public boolean sendMessage(String message)
      Send a message over the network. The message will be sent as a single line. The message should not contain any newlines.
      Parameters:
      message - the message to send
      Returns:
      true if the message was sent successfully, false if the connection was closed

    • close

      protected void close()
      Close the network connection. This will also cause the thread that receives messages to stop.

    • handleStart

      protected void handleStart()
      Handles a start of the connection. This is invoked when the reading thread is started.

    • handleMessage

      protected abstract void handleMessage(String message)
      Handles a message received from the connection.
      Parameters:
      message - the message received from the connection

    • handleDisconnect

      protected abstract void handleDisconnect()
      Handles a disconnect from the connection, i.e., when the connection is closed.
